---
format: revealjs
---

## Today's Agenda {background-image="Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

```{r}
# background-size="1920px 1080px"
library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(kableExtra)
```

<br>

::: {.r-fit-text}

**Final Paper Preparations**

- Applying and evaluating the models

:::

<br>

::: r-stack
Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2026)
:::

::: notes
Prep for Class

1. Review submitted work on Canvas (notes from applying models to the cases)

:::




## Final Exam (Due May 6, 10am) {background-image="Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

<br>

Select a **NEW** international political event and make an argument that one of the IR theories / models we’ve studied in class this term “best” explains the event. 

- Your paper must include a counter-argument that applies and evaluates a second theory to the case.

- No double-dipping on realism/liberalism

- At least one theory from the second half of class

- No events used in the prior paper

::: notes

**Questions on the assignment?**

<br>

For today you have each:

1. Broken your event down into its key components: Who is the actor? What is the action they performed that you are explaining?

2. Briefly explained the event using THREE of the theories from class

<br>

By the end of class today you should have:

- An international political event to explain supported by high quality evidence

- THREE competing explanations of the event using models of IR, and

- A start on the strengths and weaknesses of each model applied to your event

<br>

Today you are going to receive AND give useful feedback

- Your job will be to pitch your work to EVERYONE else in the class in order to get their evaluation of it

- **SLIDE**

:::



## {background-image="Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
**Practice & Feedback Time**

1. Is the specific action being explained clearly enough?

2. Is each theory being applied correctly?

3. Which theory do YOU believe is MOST useful?

4. Which theory do YOU believe is LEAST useful?
:::

::: notes

Your job will be to listen to everyone's proposals and then give them these four pieces of feedback

- **Questions on the task?**

- Go!

<br>

**SLIDE**: Pick your strongest two theories and submit an outline of the paper before class on Friday

:::




## For Friday: Outline {background-image="Images/background-worldmap4.png" .center .smaller}

**Intro**

- What is the question?
- Why is the question important?
- Thesis statement: Theory X is most useful because...

**Premise 1: Theory 1 is most useful because... (with evidence)**

**Premise 2: Theory 1 is most useful because... (with evidence)**

**Premise 3: Theory 1 is most useful because... (with evidence)**

**Counter-Argument: Theory 2**

- What are the strengths of this explanation?
- What are the weaknesses of this explanation?
- Why is it not ultimately more useful than Theory 1?

::: notes

Outline due in class on Friday

- Submit as an assignment, NOT a Canvas discussion posting

- Bring your outline to class

<br>

### Questions?

- Get to it!
:::
